rulings.Case Title: IT BRIX LLC VS. MICHAEL ENGMANN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs It Brix Llc And George Athannassov'S Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, September 27, 2013, Line 25, Plaintiffs IT Brix LLC And George Athannassov's Motion For Summary Adjudication As to the parties' cross motions for summary adjudication under CCP Section 437c(s), the Court finds as follows: As to Issue One, summary adjudication is granted in favor of Plaintiff ITBrix. The undisputed facts show that Plaintiff timely requested a list of Current Owners under Section 18 of the Termination Agreement, and Defendant Blogtronix did not provide an accurate list of Current Owners in accordance with Section 18. As to Issue Two, summary adjudication is granted in favor of ITBrix. Blogtronix has failed to produce sufficient evidence that the liquidated damages clause was unreasonable at the time the Termination Agreement was made. Blogtronix shall pay $150,000 in accordance with the Section 21 of the Termination Agreement. As to Issue Three, summary adjudication is granted in favor of Blogtronix. The plain language of Section 11c of the Termination Agreement states that all claims are released except for claims arising form the Termination Agreement, the License Agreement, and the Software Development Agreement. Each of Blogtronix's proposed cross-claims arise from these agreements. As to Issue Four, a triable issue of fact exists as to whether Kendu Partners is an "Outside Investor" under the terms of Section 17 of the Termination Agreement. Kendu has produced a document showing that ownership interest was transferred from Blogtronix to Kendu Partners in December 2006, which is outside the Investment Restriction Period in the Termination Agreement. (See Blogtronix's Evidence in Support of Opposition to ITBrix's Motion for Summary Adjudication, Exh. D.) As to Issue Five, a triable issue of fact exists as to what damages are payable to ITBrix as the result of the transfer of ownership. Blogtronix has produced evidence to create a triable issue of fact as to whether the transfer of interest occurred outside the Investment Restriction Period in the Termination Agreement. (Id.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/LCN)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID W. GUERDAT VS. SALLY A. UNTERBERGER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Complaince With Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents And Things
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 27, 2013, Line 2: Defendant SALLY UNTERBERGER'S Motion To Compel Compliance With Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents And Things Pro Tem Judge William Shapiro, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. Hearing required. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THEODORE GLENWRIGHT
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Petition For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, September 27, 2013, Line 4, PETITIONER THEODORE GLENWRIGHT'S Notice Of Motion And Petition For Writ Of Mandate The petition for writ of mandate is denied. The controlling statute is Vehicle Code § 13353.3(b)(2)(A) and therefore, the one year suspension was proper. rulings.Case Title: ERNICE FAGAN VS. KING AMERICAN AMBULANCE SERVICE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, September 27, 2013, Line 15, DEFENDANT ALLIED MEDICAL SERVICES DBA DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend under CCP 430.10(e). The complaint fails to state a cause of action. Plaintiff lacks standing to maintain this action under CCP 367 because she is not the real party in interest. Plaintiff complains about an injury to Mr. Baumel, but does not allege facts showing how she is his successor and can maintain this action. The alleged common law marriage does not create standing unless Plaintiff can allege that the common law marriage was validly contracted in another state. See Knight v. Superior Court (2005) 128 Cal. App. 4th 14, 19. The durable power of attorney does not create standing. The Court rejects defendant's statute of limitations argument. The complaint does not clearly disclose the date of injury. While defendant appeared at the premises on April 27, 2011, it is unclear when the injury (i.e., Mr. Baumel's death) occurred. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/LCN)

rulings.Case Title: KEITH POTTER VS. VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, September 27, 2013, Line 18, DEFENDANT THE CHRIS JAMES CORPORATION DBA VANGUARD'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT The Court sustains the demurrer with 20 days leave to amend as to causes of action 1-5. Plaintiff has leave to allege the nature of his disability more clearly. The demurrer is overruled as to causes of action 6 and 7 because the amended complaint does not facially disclose that the professional exemption applies to plaintiff. The demurrer is overruled as to cause of action 8 because the Court takes plaintiff's allegation regarding willfulness as true. Defendant's demurrers to causes of action 6-8 raise factual issues. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/LCN)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL G. SCHINNER VS. RAYMOND ZHANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, September 27, 2013, Line 19, DEFENDANT HERSHORN & HENRY, LLP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT Denied. The act of giving funds to a person does not fall within the ambit of a special motion to strike.
